summary: The diff implements client-side audio feature extraction and genre classification, removing the backend actor dependency from GenreClassifier.tsx. However, REQ-8 explicitly requires TensorFlow.js inference with a pre-trained GTZAN-style model loaded from a CDN or bundled weights. Instead, the implementation uses a purely rule-based scoring system with no TensorFlow.js or neural network model, which fails that specific acceptance criterion. REQ-9 appears fully satisfied.

missing_requirements.requirement: Run inference with TensorFlow.js using a pre-trained GTZAN-style genre classification model loaded from a public CDN or bundled weights.
missing_requirements.reason: The diff introduces genreClassifier.ts which uses a hand-crafted rule-based scoring system with softmax normalization and pseudo-noise. There is no TensorFlow.js import, no model loading from a CDN or bundled weights, and no neural network inference. The acceptance criterion 'A TensorFlow.js model is loaded client-side and produces genre prediction results' is not met.

hallucinated_features.description: Rule-based genre scoring system with pseudo-noise injection instead of TensorFlow.js model inference
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request explicitly requires TensorFlow.js with a pre-trained model. The diff instead implements a deterministic rule-based classifier with sigmoid/softmax scoring and synthetic noise to simulate variability, which was not requested and contradicts the specified approach.
